practice.name: Problem 14 (1963 AHSME)
practice.statement: Consider the equations $x^2 + kx + 6 = 0$ and $x^2 - kx + 6 = 0$. If, when the roots of the equations are suitably listed, each root of the second equation is $5$ more than the corresponding root of the first equation, then $k$ equals

practice.name: Problem 24 (2019 AMC 10A)
practice.statement: Let $p$, $q$, and $r$ be the distinct roots of the polynomial $x^3 - 22x^2 + 80x - 67$. It is given that there exist real numbers $A$, $B$, and $C$ such that $$\frac{1}{s^3 - 22s^2 + 80s - 67} = \frac{A}{s-p} + \frac{B}{s-q} + \frac{C}{s-r}$$ for all $s \notin \{p, q, r\}$. What is $\dfrac{1}{A} + \dfrac{1}{B} + \dfrac{1}{C}$?
